LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT BRIAR TAZUK'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 8, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3,4,9, AND 10. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1,2,5,6 AND 7 IF PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD IN GOOD FAITH THAT DEFENDANT IS DEARHEART'S ALTER EGO OR OWED A DUTY DIRECTLY TO PLAINTIFF. LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANT BRIAR TAZUK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS ON THE BASIS OF WHICH TAZUK WOULD OWE A FIDUCIARY OR NEGLIGENCE DUTY TO PLAINTIFF BASED ON CONDUCT POST-DATING THE RELEASE AND ON A THEORY THAT WOULD NOT BE BARRED BY THE DMA BANKRUPTCY; TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED SPECIFICITY; AND TO FILE A COMPLAINT WITH THE EXHIBITS ATTACHED. RULING:
ARGUED and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT BRIAR TAZUK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS ON THE BASIS OF WHICH TAZUK WOULD OWE A FIDUCIARY OR NEGLIGENCE DUTY TO PLAINTIFF BASED ON CONDUCT POST-DATING THE RELEASE AND ON A THEORY THAT WOULD NOT BE BARRED BY THE DMA BANKRUPTCY; TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED SPECIFICITY; AND TO FILE A COMPLAINT WITH THE EXHIBITS ATTACHED. Moving party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR # 2737 (301/PJB)
